title: select-best-source keeps only one file per (competition, season), dropping the rest
evidence: src/brazilian_soccer/data.clj:160 select-best-source picks the single source with the most rows per (competition,season); complementary non-overlapping fixtures in the discarded file are silently dropped
suggestion: Acceptable tradeoff to avoid double-counting, but consider fuzzy fixture-level dedup so non-overlapping matches from secondary sources are retained

title: BR-Football-Dataset tournaments other than Copa do Brasil pass through as raw labels
evidence: src/brazilian_soccer/data.clj:102 competition-from-tournament only maps 'copa do brasil'; any other tournament value becomes the raw (str t), so competition filters may not match canonical labels
suggestion: Map the remaining tournament strings onto canonical competition labels (or normalize the filter side)

title: code_quality score 0.83 (not 1.0)
evidence: scores.json code_quality=0.8333 — minor style/quality deductions from the quality scorer; no specific defect identified in review
suggestion: Run clj-kondo to surface the specific lint hits if a perfect quality score is desired

title: Capabilities beyond the spec: venue/opponent/limit filters, season standings tie-breaks, statistics tool
evidence: src/brazilian_soccer/queries.clj:38 find-matches supports :venue/:opponent/:limit; standings sorts by points then goal-difference then goals-for (queries.clj:174); mcp.clj exposes a dedicated statistics tool
suggestion: None — surfaced as positive signal, not a deduction
